bid_claims.quote_text: Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.

On July 21, 2013, Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.
On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock
the $16 per share of common stock in cash to be paid to Saks’ shareholders in the proposed merger

event_claims.description: Sponsor A made an unsolicited contact expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Mr. Sadove and Richard Baker discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.

event_claims.description: The board directed management to continue exploring potential transactions.
event_claims.quote_text: The board directed Mr. Sadove and Saks management to continue exploring these potential transactions.

event_claims.description: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor A.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 26, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with each of Sponsor A and Sponsor E, who were considering participating in a potential joint acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor E.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 26, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with each of Sponsor A and Sponsor E, who were considering participating in a potential joint acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Hudson’s Bay.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 30, 2013, Saks and Hudson’s Bay entered into a confidentiality agreement.

event_claims.description: The board authorized implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with a potential acquiror could be reached.
event_claims.quote_text: authorized (1) the implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with one of the potential acquirors which had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached on terms that would offer attractive value to Saks shareholders

event_claims.description: Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details requesting offers by July 11, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 2, 2013, at the direction of Saks, Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E, with a request for submission of offers for an all-cash acquisition of Saks, along with comments on the draft merger agreement, no later than July 11, 2013.

event_claims.description: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 8, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay submitted a proposal expressing continued interest in acquiring Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Sponsor E together with Sponsor G submitted a joint proposal expressing continued interest in acquiring Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: The board authorized Goldman Sachs to tell Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor E/Sponsor A that their initial price proposals were insufficient.
event_claims.quote_text: The board authorized Goldman Sachs to inform each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E and Sponsor A, on the other hand, that their initial price proposals were insufficient

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs it was prepared to offer $16 per share, subject to negotiating a definitive binding agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock

event_claims.description: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.
event_claims.quote_text: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.

event_claims.description: The board approved the merger and merger agreement and recommended shareholder approval.
event_claims.quote_text: Following extensive discussion, the board unanimously agreed to approve the merger and the merger agreement, and the transactions contemplated thereby, substantially in the form presented to the board, and to recommend the approval of the merger agreement by Saks’ shareholders.

event_claims.description: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents.
event_claims.quote_text: Following the board’s approval of the merger and the merger agreement, Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: During the week of June 3, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs spoke with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E and were informed that Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).
On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
